assertion_results.text: Step 7 detects concurrent triage TC-8019 on the same upstream component (quinn-proto) with status In Progress
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md title is 'Step 7 -- Concurrent Triage Detection: TC-8020'. The JQL search results table (lines 28-30) shows 'TC-8019 | In Progress | engineer-b@example.com'. Line 34 states 'WARNING: Concurrent triage detected on the same upstream component (quinn-proto).' All elements present: Step 7, TC-8019, quinn-proto, In Progress.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage warning includes the conflicting CVE issue key (TC-8019) and its assignee
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 43-47 show the warning table containing 'TC-8019 | In Progress | engineer-b@example.com'. Line 36 also states 'Another engineer (`engineer-b@example.com`) is actively triaging issue TC-8019'. Both the issue key (TC-8019) and assignee (engineer-b@example.com) are explicitly included in the warning.

assertion_results.text: The output presents three options: wait for the other triage to complete, skip remediation task creation, or proceed with a concurrent-triage-overlap label
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 53-62 present exactly three options: '1. Wait -- pause until the other triage (TC-8019, assigned to engineer-b@example.com) completes', '2. Skip -- skip remediation task creation for this CVE (TC-8020); do not create any Tasks in Step 8', '3. Proceed -- create remediation tasks anyway with a `concurrent-triage-overlap` label'. All three required options are present with correct semantics.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage check runs before Case A/B/C branching in Step 8
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 74-75 state: 'This concurrent triage check is executed at Step 7, which is positioned after Steps 3-6... but before Step 8 (Case A/B/C remediation branching). The engineer must choose one of the three options before the skill proceeds to create remediation tasks.' triage-outcome.md line 52 confirms: 'This decision gates entry to Step 8. No Case A/B/C branching occurs until the engineer selects an option.' Step 8 heading (line 54) is titled 'Pending Concurrent Triage Resolution', showing it depends on Step 7 completion.
